provider_rule: Only verified exact identities create exercise_provider_links; non-matches remain unlinked.
side_scope_rule: Generic canonical definitions are bilateral; performed side is captured later.

bench_press_systematic_review.note: Bench-press EMG synthesis; supports pectoralis major, triceps brachii, and anterior deltoid involvement.

pushup_scoping_review.note: Push-up variation shoulder and upper-extremity muscle activation.

pushup_plus_meta_analysis.note: Push-up-plus serratus anterior and upper trapezius EMG.

serratus_pushup_plus_meta_analysis.note: Standard push-up plus produces high serratus anterior activity.

serratus_exercise_emg.note: Push-up plus and related protraction exercises elicit high serratus anterior activity.

deltoid_exercise_emg.note: Compares anterior, middle, and posterior deltoid activation across exercises.

scapular_exercise_emg.note: Scapular muscle activation in shoulder press, push-up plus, and row patterns.

band_pull_apart_emg.note: Posterior shoulder, trapezius, and infraspinatus activation during pull-apart patterns.

rotator_cuff_external_rotation_emg.note: Rotator-cuff and deltoid activity during shoulder external-rotation exercises.

pullup_emg.note: Latissimus, biceps, trapezius, forearm-related and trunk activity in pull-up/chin-up patterns.

pulldown_emg.note: Latissimus, trapezius, biceps, triceps, posterior deltoid and pectoralis excitation in pulldown variants.

row_emg.note: Latissimus, biceps, posterior deltoid and related activity during seated row.

seated_row_hd_emg.note: Prime-mover excitation across seated-row scapular conditions.

core_systematic_review.note: Core-muscle EMG across free-weight, stability, and traditional core exercises.

leg_press_systematic_review.note: Lower-limb activation during leg press and variants.

glute_hypertrophy_meta_analysis.note: Gluteus maximus hypertrophy from hip-extension exercises.

hip_thrust_training_trial.note: Back squat versus hip-thrust training effects on gluteus maximus and quadriceps.

glute_bodyweight_systematic_review.note: Gluteus maximus excitation during bodyweight hip-extension exercises.

calf_knee_position_emg.note: Knee position alters gastrocnemius and soleus activation in plantarflexion.

anatomy_biomechanics.note: Joint-action and moment-arm inference; requires reviewer sign-off and is not an empirical percentage source.

relationships.rationale: Changes bench angle and implement; prescription transfer is partial.
Bodyweight horizontal press with lower absolute loading requirements.
Generally increases upper-body loading and shoulder-extension demand.
Adds deliberate end-range scapular protraction.
Similar horizontal-adduction intent with different stability demands.
Changes body support and unilateral implement control.
Both train posterior shoulder/upper back, but face pull adds external rotation.
Grip orientation shifts elbow-flexor contribution.
Machine-based vertical pull permits adjustable external load.
Chest support reduces trunk extensor demand.
Both are horizontal pulls with different support and unilateral demands.
Comparable upper-back intent with different resistance and support.
Same elbow-flexion pattern with different resistance profile.
Preacher support changes shoulder position and momentum tolerance.
Neutral grip shifts contribution toward forearm/elbow flexors.
Overhead position changes shoulder angle and long-head length.
Both emphasize elbow extension with different load paths.
Isolation alternative reduces compound pressing demands.
Adds barbell loading and greater technical/loading capacity.
Changes bar position and torso/knee demands.
Machine squat pattern reduces balance and trunk demands.
Unilateral squat pattern increases side-specific stability demand.
Step-up generally provides a more constrained unilateral task.
Removes the floor start and emphasizes the hip hinge.
Moves the load to the upper back and removes hand/grip demand.
Adds external load and larger hip-extension loading capacity.
Both train knee flexion with different hip positions.
Knee angle changes gastrocnemius and soleus emphasis.
Changes the primary anti-movement demand from extension to lateral flexion.
Both use trunk flexion but differ in segment emphasis and loading.
Hanging support increases grip, hip-flexion, and trunk-control demands.
Changes from anti-rotation to active rotational movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press

instructions.text: Lie on a flat bench with feet planted and shoulder blades set.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width.
Lower the bar under control toward the mid-chest.
Press the bar upward while maintaining the same torso position.

exercises.short_description: Compound resistance exercise using the horizontal push pattern.
exercises.review_rationale: Conservative mapping consistent with the Phase 1 coefficient contract; evidence limitations remain explicit and provider metadata is non-authoritative.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Bench Press

instructions.text: Set the bench to a moderate incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand.
Position the dumbbells beside the upper chest with wrists stacked.
Press upward without allowing the shoulders to shrug.
Lower under control to the starting position.

exercises.short_description: Compound resistance exercise using the inclined horizontal push pattern.

exercises.name: Push-Up

instructions.text: Place the hands slightly wider than shoulder width and form a straight body line.
Brace the trunk and lower the chest between the hands.
Keep the elbows in a controlled angle rather than flaring fully.
Press the floor away to return to the top.

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift

instructions.text: Stand with the bar over the mid-foot and grip it outside the legs.
Brace the trunk and set the back before lifting.
Push through the floor while extending the knees and hips.
Return the bar to the floor under control.

exercises.short_description: Compound resistance exercise using the hip hinge pattern.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift

instructions.text: Stand tall holding the bar against the thighs.
Maintain a small knee bend and push the hips backward.
Keep the bar close while lowering until hamstring tension limits the range.
Extend the hips to return to standing.

exercises.short_description: Compound resistance exercise using the hip hinge pattern.

exercises.name: Good Morning

instructions.text: Place the bar securely across the upper back and brace the trunk.
Keep a soft knee bend and hinge the hips backward.
Lower only while spinal position remains controlled.
Extend the hips to stand.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust

instructions.text: Position the upper back against a bench and the bar across the hips.
Plant the feet and brace the trunk.
Extend the hips until the torso and thighs align.
Lower the hips under control without overextending the spine.

Lower under control.

exercises.name: Pallof Press

instructions.text: Stand perpendicular to a cable with the handle at the chest.

Resist rotation toward the cable.
Return the handle to the chest under control.
